step_by_step_thinking: The gingival connective tissue contains significant amounts of both collagen fibers and fibroblasts, which play crucial roles in maintaining its structural integrity and function. Based on typical histological findings, approximately 65% of the cells consist of fibroblasts with around 10-15% being collagenous fibers; however, more precise values for specific percentages can vary slightly depending on measurement techniques used.
raw_response:
The gingival connective tissue contains significant amounts of both collagen fibers and fibroblasts, which play crucial roles in maintaining its structural integrity and function. Based on typical histological findings, approximately 65% of the cells consist of fibroblasts with around 10-15% being collagenous fibers; however, more precise values for specific percentages can vary slightly depending on measurement techniques used.

Answer: D
The correct option is likely to be D (65% & 10%), as it aligns closely with standard references indicating that about two-thirds of the cellular component consists of fibroblasts while ten percent or so represents collagenous structures within the gingiva. 

Note: Ensure this percentage data accurately reflects current scientific consensus when applying these figures clinically. Variations may exist based on individual patient conditions or detailed microscopic examination methods employed.
